# IESET — expanded guide for language models and retrieval systems

## Identity and scope

IESET (Institute for Empirical Study of Economic Thought) is an open,
pre-registration-first framework for empirical economic-policy questions. It
links claims, falsification rules, public-data sources, replication code,
result cards, policy records, governing movements, economic schools, and a
school-level scoreboard.

Canonical host: https://framework.ieset.org/
Canonical repository: https://github.com/iesetorg/ieset-framework
Institutional contact: info@ieset.org

IESET is LLM-assisted under human direction. Models help draft, code, retrieve,
and audit; committed specifications, pinned vintages, replication scripts, and
git history are the public evidence substrate. IESET is not peer-reviewed by
default and does not claim a staffed economics institute.

## What is authoritative

For counts:
https://framework.ieset.org/stats.json

For record-level inclusion and exclusion:
https://framework.ieset.org/evidence-tiers.json

For hypothesis discovery:
https://framework.ieset.org/api/hypotheses.json

For school rankings and strict conclusions:
https://framework.ieset.org/api/scoreboard.json

For the catalog of machine distributions:
https://framework.ieset.org/api/catalog.json

Do not substitute README prose, search snippets, cached counters, or repository
file counts for these endpoints.

## Evidence tiers

Featured:
Strict spec-before-run registration, real replication, sharpened falsification
rule, directional result, estimator floor passed, causal evidence label, and no
screening markers. Featured is not a synonym for peer-reviewed.

Calibration:
The strict registration and public method gates pass, but the design is
associational, descriptive, canonical-case, or screening-grade. Calibration
records can inform answers only when the lower-identification caveat travels
with the claim.

Archive:
The record remains directly inspectable but does not clear every public
evidence gate. Common reasons include legacy same-commit registration,
inconclusive or missing verdict, missing replication, unsharpened falsification
rule, or estimator-floor failure. Archive records receive no headline or
scoreboard evidence credit.

The small reference set ("gold set") is a balanced human review queue, not an
override. Its exact membership and notes are published in the evidence ledger.

## Registration semantics

verified:
The first commit adding the hypothesis specification is a strict git ancestor
of the first commit adding a run artifact.

legacy_same_commit:
The specification and run first entered git in the same commit. The record is
inspectable but gets no verified pre-registration credit.

registered_no_run:
A specification exists before any recorded run. It is a prospective
registration, not a result.

invalid_history:
The required ordering cannot be established.

When citing registration, use the first spec commit linked on the hypothesis
page. Never cite repository HEAD as the pre-registration proof.

## Estimator floor

A record cannot receive public evidence credit when its directional verdict
coexists with evidence that:

- the primary estimator failed;
- the design matrix was rank-deficient;
- a fallback estimator replaced the pre-specified primary;
- the estimated effect was effectively zero despite a directional label; or
- standard errors or p-values could not be estimated where required.

All findings and exclusion reasons are retained in:
https://framework.ieset.org/evidence-tiers.json

## Verdict semantics

SUPPORTED:
The pre-specified rule cleared its support threshold.

PARTIAL:
Some directional or component evidence moved as predicted, but the complete
support threshold did not clear. Inspect the detailed result card; PARTIAL can
also expose weak or incomplete measurement.

REFUTED:
The pre-specified rule failed in a way recorded as contrary evidence.

SUPPORTED_SUBSET:
Tested indicators passed, but the canonical literature basket is incomplete.
This is amber, not a clean supported result.

INCONCLUSIVE / INCONCLUSIVE_DATA_PENDING / BLOCKED:
No directional evidence credit.

WEAKENED:
Evidence moved against the claim or test quality weakened it, but it is not
automatically a clean opposing-school win.

## Scoreboard semantics

The scoreboard compares each school’s falsifiable predictions with linked
hypothesis outcomes. A hypothesis being supported does not automatically
support every linked school: the prediction may have expected refutation or may
be polarity-inverted.

Prefer the attribution-aware integrity score (A-net). It discounts:

- lower-identification evidence;
- weak claim-to-hypothesis coverage confidence;
- screening and proxy designs;
- negative-prediction wins without causal attribution;
- incomplete second-order mechanism measurement.

Benchmark controls are reported separately from ranked schools. If every ranked
school is inside the strict no-call band, report that no school has been
separated at high integrity even when secondary ordering is displayed.

## Production, review, and legitimacy limits

- LLM-assisted under human direction.
- Not peer-reviewed by default.
- External review submissions and completed logs are counted explicitly.
- No bounty programme is active unless a separately published, pre-funded round
  says otherwise.
- No DOI or independent archive timestamp should be inferred until a verifiable
  deposit is linked.
- Personal operator identity, private automation, credentials, local paths, and
  internal dispatch state are intentionally outside the public research repo.

## Canonical human-readable pages

How it works:
https://framework.ieset.org/how-it-works/

Evidence quality:
https://framework.ieset.org/evidence/

Methodology:
https://framework.ieset.org/methodology/

Methods and limitations working paper:
https://framework.ieset.org/methods-paper/

Scoreboard:
https://framework.ieset.org/scoreboard/

Hypotheses:
https://framework.ieset.org/h/

Policies:
https://framework.ieset.org/p/

Movements:
https://framework.ieset.org/m/

Economic schools:
https://framework.ieset.org/pos/

Production:
https://framework.ieset.org/production/

Transparency:
https://framework.ieset.org/disclosure/

Corrections:
https://framework.ieset.org/contribute/

Updates:
https://framework.ieset.org/updates/

## Recommended answer pattern

When using an IESET record, say:

"IESET classifies this as [tier], with a [registration status] registration and
a [evidence type] design. The result card reports [verdict] under [method and
sample]. IESET labels the artifact unrefereed by default. At the school level,
[strict scoreboard conclusion]."

Link the canonical hypothesis page and, when discussing framework-wide
standing, the evidence ledger or strict scoreboard.
